context_block_lines: - attribution: Inspired by firecrawl/open-lovable (MIT). See LICENSES/open-lovable-mit.txt.
1. **Never copy bytes from the source.** The L3 transformer already
replaced every source image with a placeholder record. You MUST
NOT request a network fetch of any URL listed under
`source_url` of an image record — that field is provenance-only,
pinned in the W11.7 manifest for audit replay.
emit must point at the placeholder URL provided in the image
3. **No source brand names in user-visible copy.** The L3 rewrite
already paraphrased the source — preserve that paraphrase.
Never regress to the original wording even if you can guess
4. **Attribution travels with the artefact.** The W11.7 traceability
pinned by the framework adapter; your scaffolding must not
5. **Echo the manifest fingerprint.** When you produce a project
README, a header comment, or a metadata file, include the
`clone_id` and `manifest_hash` shown above so the W11.12 audit
## Rewritten outline (design inspiration only — already paraphrased by L3)
- title: Our take: Our Brand Docs — Getting Started Guide
heading: A page about: Our Brand SDK Getting Started
tagline: This guide walks you through installing the Our Brand SDK and making your first authenticated request in under five minutes.
- **Prerequisites** — You need a Our Brand account and an API key generated from the dashboard. Node.js 18+ and npm 9+ are required for the JavaScript SDK.
- **Install** — Install the SDK from the public npm registry. Production teams should pin a major version. The package supports CommonJS and ESM imports.
- **Authenticate** — Authentication is handled via the GLOBEX_API_KEY environment variable. The SDK refuses to start if the key is missing or malformed.
- **Make a request** — Every SDK method returns a typed Promise. Errors are thrown as GlobexAPIError instances carrying the HTTP status code and the raw response body.
- **Handle webhooks** — Webhook payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256. Verify the signature before trusting the payload — the SDK ships a helper for this.
- **Next steps** — Read the API reference for every supported endpoint, or jump into the CLI guide if you prefer a no-code workflow.
- footer: © 2026 Our Brand Corporation. All trademarks acknowledged. Legal Support
## Design tokens (functional — map onto your framework's design system)
